prompt: Write four absurd but coherent sentences in the voice of a confused medieval chronicler who has been hired to document the Q3 results of a modern SaaS company.
response:
# Chronicle of the Quarterly Reckoning, Year of Our Lord 2024

Verily, I have been tasked by this merchant guild of "software-as-a-service" to record how their invisible wares—which exist not in form nor substance, yet cost great sums—did increase by some twenty and seven percent in the third quarter, a miracle I attribute to either divine intervention or the dark arts. The ledgers speak of "customer acquisition costs" and "churn rates," terms most perplexing, as though they wage war upon their own patrons and must purchase them anew each season like serfs traded at market. Their treasury swells with "recurring revenue," an enchantment wherein the same gold coin doth arrive each month by phantom courier, sparing them the honest toil of earning fresh coin through actual goods. I confess I understand not whether these people are merchants, sorcerers, or merely collectors of promises, yet the numbers ascend and the lords are pleased, so I have dutifully inscribed these mysteries upon parchment for posterity's befuddlement.
